content.problemStatement:
Construct an N×MN \times MN×M matrix with entries having **positive** integers such that:


If M>1M \gt 1M>1, ithi^{th}ith row is **strictly** increasing from left to right with a fixed common difference did_idi​ for all 1≤i≤N1\le i \le N1≤i≤N.
If N>1N \gt 1N>1, jthj^{th}jth column is **strictly** increasing from top to bottom with a fixed common difference cjc_jcj​ for all 1≤j≤M1\le j \le M1≤j≤M.
**All** the common differences should be **distinct**.
In the case of multiple answers, find an arrangement that minimizes the **maximum** element of the matrix.

Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, two space-separated integers NNN and MMM - the dimensions of the matrix.


Output Format
For each test case, output NNN lines with each line having MMM space-separated integers representing the answer.
constraints: Sum N⋅MN \cdot MN⋅M over all cases is atmost 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The values d1,d2,d_1, d_2,d1​,d2​, and d3d_3d3​ are 1,3,1, 3,1,3, and 555 respectively. The values c1,c2,c_1, c_2,c1​,c2​, and c3c_3c3​ are 2,4,2, 4,2,4, and 666 respectively. Note that all these common differences {d1,d2,d3,c1,c2,c3}\{d_1, d_2, d_3, c_1, c_2, c_3\}{d1​,d2​,d3​,c1​,c2​,c3​} are distinct. Moreover, it can be shown that over all valid matrices, 151515 is the minimal value of the maximum element of the matrix one can achieve.
Test case 222: The values d1d_1d1​ is 111. It can be shown that over all valid matrices, 222 is the minimal value of the maximum element of the matrix one can achieve.
